sections.paragraph:
εἰλικρινῶς adv. of εἰλικρινής (Pla.; Epict. 4, 2, 6; OGI 441, 5; Michel 394, 48; Philo; Albinos, Didasc. 11; Ath., R. 54, 28) sincerely, candidly κατανοεῖν consider 1 Cl 32:1.

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

Michel Michel = CM., Recueil d’inscriptions grecques—List 3

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 282
